articles.title: Engadget Podcast: Who needs Valve's Steam Machine?
articles.description: This week on the Engadget Podcast, we've got our first impressions of Valve's pricey Steam Machine, and we chat about Sony dumping PlayStation discs in 2028.
articles.content: This week on the Engadget Podcast, we've got our first impressions of Valve's pricey Steam Machine, and we chat about Sony dumping PlayStation discs in 2028.
articles.author: staff@engadget.com (Devindra Hardawar)

articles.description: Ryan welcomes Benny Chen, co-founder of Fireworks AI, to the show to explore what actually makes an AI application good or not, how to balance qualitative signals with quantitative metrics when evaluating AI, and how open-source eval protocols and community efforts are setting the standard for AI ev
articles.content: Ryan welcomes Benny Chen, co-founder of Fireworks AI, to the show to explore what actually makes an AI application good or not, how to balance qualitative signals with quantitative metrics when evaluating AI, and how open-source eval protocols and community efforts are setting the standard for AI evaluation.вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌвЂЊвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌ
articles.author: Phoebe Sajor

articles.title: I finally got my Trump phone
articles.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. One year on, our phones have finally arrived. 12 months, 16 days, 21 hours, and 54 minutes after I first heard about Trump Mobile's T1 Phone 8002 (gold version), I'm finally holding one in my own hands. That's right, The Verge
articles.content: Unlike most phones, the T1 still ships with a charger. Trump Mobile seemingly isnвЂ™t too worried about e-waste. Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week . One year on, our phones have finally arrived. 12 months, 16 days, 21 hours, and 54 minutes after I first heard about Trump Mobile's T1 Phone 8002 (gold version), I'm finally holding one in my own hands. That's right, The Verge 's Trump phones have arrived. And yes, I do mean phones - we have three of them: We ord

articles.title: The Right Way to Pair AI With Terraform Plans
articles.description: terraform plan is honest about what it's going to do. The problem is it's also verbose, repetitive, and full of cosmetic changes (like recomputed tags) mixed in with real ones (like a database instance scheduled for -/+ replace ). On a 400-line plan, the dangerous changes hide. This is the kind of t
articles.content: terraform plan is honest about what it's going to do. The problem is it's also verbose, repetitive, and full of cosmetic changes (like recomputed tags) mixed in with real ones (like a database instance scheduled for -/+ replace ). On a 400-line plan, the dangerous changes hide. This is the kind of task AI is actually good at: skimming structured text, flagging the entries that matter, ignoring the rest. But "paste plan into Claude" is not the workflow. There's a specific shape to this that works
articles.author: James Joyner

articles.title: This slim camera has a transparent LCD screen for a viewfinder
articles.description: Despite the fact that smartphones have become impressively capable shooters, standalone point-and-shoot cameras are enjoying a renaissance. The tiny Kodak Charmera is still wildly popular, while influencers are scrambling to find aging Canon cameras on eBay. Godox, a company best known for its photo
articles.content: Despite the fact that smartphones have become impressively capable shooters, standalone point-and-shoot cameras are enjoying a renaissance. The tiny Kodak Charmera is still wildly popular , while influencers are scrambling to find aging Canon cameras on eBay . Godox, a company best known for its photography lighting products, is the latest to join the simple camera craze, but its new C100 stands out from the crowd by skipping a color preview screen for a transparent LCD that doubles as an optica
articles.author: Andrew Liszewski

articles.title: UPS never required inspection of part that failed before engine flew off plane jet

articles.title: I Built an AI Tool That Finds Wasted Cloud Spending вЂ” And Its Carbon Footprint. Published: True
articles.description: The difficulty If youвЂ™re using Google Cloud, youвЂ™re probably paying for stuff you donвЂ™t need anymoreвЂ”a server someone forgot to turn off, a storage disk someone forgot to delete, or an IP address someone forgot to release. "That waste is costing us money." It consumes electricity. It generates carbo
articles.content: The difficulty If youвЂ™re using Google Cloud, youвЂ™re probably paying for stuff you donвЂ™t need anymoreвЂ”a server someone forgot to turn off, a storage disk someone forgot to delete, or an IP address someone forgot to release. "That waste is costing us money." It consumes electricity. It generates carbon emissions. Almost nobody tracks those alongside the cost. I wanted a tool that could find both problems simultaneously, without me having to think. So I made one. Live demo: https://greenops-dashboa
articles.author: Raghu Putta

articles.description: Vivek Raghunathan, SVP of engineering at Snowflake, joins Leaders of Code at Snowflake Summit to break down the five-stage framework his org used to go from "let chaos reign" to a repeatable, org-wide system for AI-assisted engineering.вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊ
articles.content: Vivek Raghunathan, SVP of engineering at Snowflake, joins Leaders of Code at Snowflake Summit to break down the five-stage framework his org used to go from "let chaos reign" to a repeatable, org-wide system for AI-assisted engineering.вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌвЂЊвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊ
articles.author: Eira May

articles.title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
articles.description: ItвЂ™s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as theyвЂ™ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the newly-transplanted eye wasnвЂ™t able to see. But researchers believe they might have a solution: a devi
articles.content: ItвЂ™s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as theyвЂ™ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago , the newly-transplanted eye wasnвЂ™t able to see. But researchers believe they might have a solution: a device that maintains and revives freshly removed eyeballs using a technique called perfusion. Perfusion works by providing surgically-removed organs with some of the oxygen and nutrients they typically
articles.author: Jessica Hamzelou

articles.title: While youвЂ™re watching the World Cup, the feds may be watching you
articles.description: It's a big year for America. It's the semiquincentennial, otherwise known as America250, and the United States is cohosting the World Cup. But spectators at these events - and the millions of people who live in the cities hosting them - may not realize that they, too, are being watched. From Kansas
articles.content: It's a big year for America. It's the semiquincentennial, otherwise known as America250, and the United States is cohosting the World Cup . But spectators at these events - and the millions of people who live in the cities hosting them - may not realize that they, too, are being watched. From Kansas City to New York, the US cities hosting the World Cup have been ramping up their surveillance capabilities in the months leading up to the tournament. Security measures are at an all-time high in Was
articles.author: Gaby Del Valle

articles.title: The UKвЂ™s generational tobacco ban might not work. IвЂ™m supporting it anyway.
articles.description: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based homework every week. And they are both absolutely repulsed by the idea of smoking. That was not the prevaili
articles.content: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based homework every week. And they are both absolutely repulsed by the idea of smoking. That was not the prevailing sentiment when I was young. My parents smoked. The customers at our familyвЂ™s restaurant smoked. Cartoon characters smoked. My friends and I would buy little cigarette-box-shaped packets of sugary w
articles.author: Jessica Hamzelou

articles.title: Despite the darkness, I still see signs of hope in America
articles.description: It's difficult to pinpoint the moment in my life where America started to lose the plot.
articles.content: The last time America celebrated a big anniversary, I was all of three years old. Even so, I retain a few fuzzy memories from a sunny summer afternoon in small-town Michigan: climbing on a cannon in front of the courthouse, watching a parade, and seeing my dad, a veteran and Centreville city councilman, giving a short talk about democracy. Only later would I realize the significance of the date: July 4th, 1976, AmericaвЂ™s bicentennial. America was imperfect and inconsistent in its approaches to "
articles.author: Eric Berger

After Apple had announced the support of OCI-compatible containers in the June '25 it took a year to complete development and implement full support of continers. Apple had published 1.0 version of own containe
articles.content: Actually it happened in June of 2025, but the process has completed recently, though. After Apple had announced the support of OCI-compatible containers in the June '25 it took a year to complete development and implement full support of continers. Apple had published 1.0 version of own container manager ( https://github.com/apple/container ). And Microsoft had announced native support of containerization without Docker in Windows 11 ( https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/wsl-container
articles.author: /u/BankApprehensive7612

articles.title: Rocket Report: Indian startup nears first launch; SpaceX's millenary milestone
articles.description: NASA awarded Rocket Lab deals for three dedicated launches using the company's Electron rocket.
articles.content: Welcome to Edition 9.01 of the Rocket Report! Back in January, I wrote about the 20 launches and landings we were most excited about in 2026. The list included things that were, at the time, officially scheduled to occur this year. I also gave my own view of the probability of each of these events actually happening before December 31. Halfway through the year, we can only count one of the events as completed, and that was NASA's Artemis II mission in April. Many are now scheduled for next year,
articles.author: Stephen Clark

articles.title: Inside the Luddite festival harnessing Gen ZвЂ™s rage against Big Tech
articles.description: New York CityвЂ™s Summer of Ludd festival is teaching people how to live offline.
articles.content: On a Sunday evening in the middle of Tompkins Square Park in New York City вЂ™s East Village, hundreds of people gather in front of a giant papier-mГўchГ© face of a woman wearing a crown. SheвЂ™s the backdrop of a play, her body made up of curtains that look like a dress but serve a dual purpose, allowing actors to scurry on and offstage. IвЂ™m here to watch a performance called вЂњ Luddite Recreations,вЂќ which is a history of the Luddite movementвЂ”a group of artisans and textile workers who resisted the ad
articles.author: Vittoria Elliott, WIRED.com

articles.title: How GitHub used secret scanning to reach inbox zero
articles.description: GitHub had 20,000+ secret scanning alerts across 15,000 repositories. Here's how we separated signal from noise, built remediation workflows, and reached inbox zero in nine months. The post How GitHub used secret scanning to reach inbox zero appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: Several years ago, GitHub Security launched an initiative to assess and improve our overall secrets hygiene. As part of that effort, we piloted the Secret Scanning capability that was under development at the time. That’s when we found more than 20,000 secrets spread across our 15,000+ repositories. The number was significantly higher than we anticipated, but it quickly became clear that success would depend on identifying which alerts represented real risk, assigning ownership, and remedi
articles.author: Natalie Guevara

articles.title: Visiting the stars (and planets, and telescopes) in VR
articles.description: Walkthrough experience includes visits to stars, exoplanets, and observatories.
articles.content: Having a computer strapped to my face for 40 minutes was one reason to feel a little sweaty. But the tour of the Universe I had just received in virtual realityвЂ”including visits to the near vicinity of the Sun, the giant black hole at the center of our galaxy, and a hellscape of an exoplanet 41 light-years distantвЂ”provided another excuse for sensing some heat. Smithsonian Starstruck: An Immersive Experience is a 40-minute astronomy walk-through. It debuted in Washington, DC, in May with solo adu
articles.author: Rob Pegoraro

articles.title: Politician who investigated spyware abuses had his phone hacked with Pegasus spyware
